Fix env var leak when running extension commands with -e flag (#7035)

* Fix environment variable leak in extension commands with -e flag

Co-authored-by: jongio <2163001+jongio@users.noreply.github.com>

* Redesign: use GlobalCommandOptions for environment flag parsing

Instead of scattered parseEnvFlagFromArgs + DisableFlagParsing checks,
follow the established pattern for --cwd/--debug/--no-prompt:

1. Add EnvironmentName to GlobalCommandOptions
2. Parse -e/--environment in ParseGlobalFlags() before cobra runs
3. Use globalOptions.EnvironmentName as fallback in EnvFlag DI resolver
4. Use a.globalOptions directly in extensionAction.Run() for InvokeOptions

Co-authored-by: jongio <2163001+jongio@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: honor AZURE_ENV_NAME in ParseGlobalFlags and improve test coverage

- ParseGlobalFlags now uses os.Getenv("AZURE_ENV_NAME") as default for -e flag,
  matching EnvFlag.Bind() behavior so CI pipelines setting AZURE_ENV_NAME work
  for extension commands
- Export EnvNameEnvVarName constant for cross-package reuse
- Add test cases for env var fallback, flag override, concatenated -e, multi -e
- Use exported constant in tests instead of hardcoded string
- Remove non-idiomatic "Warning:" prefix from error message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wrap long test lines to satisfy lll lint rule (125 char max)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: swap fallback ordering so context takes precedence over globalOptions

Address review feedback from @wbreza and @vhvb1989:
> 'The globalOptions fallback is placed before the context check (envFlagCtxKey).
> The context mechanism exists for workflow composition. azd up uses
> context.WithValue to propagate environment to sub-commands. Consider swapping
> the order so context takes precedence over globalOptions.'

Swapped the fallback chain in the EnvFlag DI resolver so the ordering is now:
  Cobra flag -> context value -> globalOptions
This preserves the design intent where workflow composition (azd up)
takes precedence over the pre-parsed CLI flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add Runner.Invoke env var propagation tests

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on extensions.go:258:
> "The InvokeOptions construction and mapping is an important
> part of the contract between azd and extensions. I'd like to
> see test coverage for this."

Adds TestRunnerInvoke_GlobalFlagPropagation with 3 cases:
- all global flags set (Debug, NoPrompt, Cwd, Environment)
- only environment set (verifies others absent)
- no global flags (verifies no AZD_* env vars)

Uses a mock CommandRunner to capture RunArgs and verify
that InvokeOptions fields are correctly propagated as
AZD_DEBUG, AZD_NO_PROMPT, AZD_CWD, AZD_ENVIRONMENT.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: validate environment name in ParseGlobalFlags

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on auto_install.go:678:
> "[Medium] Input Validation: Consider validating environment
> name early. The raw -e value is stored without validation.
> For extension commands, extensionAction.Run() passes it
> directly to runner.Invoke() as AZD_ENVIRONMENT in the child
> process."

Uses environment.IsValidEnvironmentName() to reject invalid
names before they reach GlobalCommandOptions. Adds test
TestParseGlobalFlags_InvalidEnvironmentName with cases for
spaces and special characters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add edge cases for -e flag parsing

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on auto_install_test.go:497:
> "[Medium] A few edge cases and cross-field assertions would
> strengthen this. 1. -e without a value to verify the
> error/behavior when the flag is at end of args. 2. Cross-field
> assertions for mixed-flag cases — assert EnableDebugLogging
> and NoPrompt are true alongside -e."

Adds:
- Cross-field assertion: verifies --debug and --no-prompt are
  correctly parsed alongside -e dev
- Edge case: -e at end of args with no value returns an error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: unexport internal.EnvNameEnvVarName to reduce confusion

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on env_flag.go:23:
> "[Low] Two exported constants with the same name and value in
> different packages (internal.EnvNameEnvVarName and
> environment.EnvNameEnvVarName) may confuse callers. Consider
> keeping it unexported if only ParseGlobalFlags (in cmd) needs
> it."

Renames internal.EnvNameEnvVarName to unexported envNameEnvVarName
since it's only used within the internal package (EnvFlag.Bind).
Updates cmd/ references to use environment.EnvNameEnvVarName instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add Lookup guard before pflag.StringP to prevent panic

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on auto_install.go:644:
> "[Low] pflag.StringP panics on duplicate names. A
> globalFlagSet.Lookup guard would protect against a future
> refactor that adds environment to CreateGlobalFlagSet()."

Wraps the StringP call in a Lookup nil-check so a future
refactor that moves the environment flag into CreateGlobalFlagSet
won't cause a panic from duplicate registration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add log line when globalOptions environment fallback fires

Addresses @wbreza's review comment on container.go:204:
> "[Low] The log says 'using default environment instead' but
> with this fallback, the resolver may actually use the
> pre-parsed -e value or AZURE_ENV_NAME. Consider adding a brief
> log line when this fallback fires to aid debugging."

Adds a log.Printf when the DI resolver falls back to
globalOptions.EnvironmentName, making it clear in debug logs
that the pre-parsed value is being used instead of the default.

fix: IC test uses proper FeedbackMetadata with 2 slots

The test_named_property_ic_reuses_shared_layout_across_objects test was
using make_bytecode_with_pool which hardcodes FeedbackMetadata::empty(),
but the bytecode uses FeedbackSlot(0) and FeedbackSlot(1). This caused
shape_load_ic to be empty, resulting in index-out-of-bounds panic.

perf: optimize string handling - interning, fast comparison, concat

- Pre-seed intern pool with ~30 well-known JS property names (length,
  prototype, constructor, etc.) to avoid first-use allocation misses
- Add str_eq_fast() for O(1) pointer-based string equality
- Optimize JsValue PartialEq and is_strictly_equal with Rc::ptr_eq
  fast path before falling back to O(n) content comparison
- Convert 8 dispatch handlers from direct constant pool String::clone
  to get_string_constant() which interns + caches Rc<str> per frame:
  StaGlobal, DeleteLookupSlot, StaLookupSlot, LdaLookupSlot,
  LdaLookupSlotInsideTypeof, LdaLookupContextSlot(InsideTypeof),
  LdaLookupGlobalSlot(InsideTypeof), LdaNamedPropertyFromSuper
- Re-seed well-known names on clear_intern_pool() for test isolation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: generalize Advisor Impact Analyzer for any environment

Remove all hardcoded references to the AKS Pet Store demo lab from the
agent's core files (subagent.yaml and knowledge base). The agent now:

- Dynamically discovers workloads across any namespace/resource group
- Supports 4 environment profiles: K8s, PaaS, Hybrid, Partially observable
- Classifies workloads by role (customer-facing, data store, broker, etc.)
- Generates impact tables from discovered inventory, not fixed service lists
- Includes fallback behavior when tools/commands are unavailable
- Handles incomplete information with explicit confidence levels

Documentation updated to present the agent as generic-first, with the
Pet Store lab preserved as a worked example in demo-flow.md and the
example report.

Files changed:
- subagent.yaml: rewritten system_prompt and handoff_description
- knowledge/impact-investigation-framework.md: expanded with env profiles
- README.md: redefined as environment-agnostic
- demo-flow.md: generic prompts, lab section separated
- advisor-impact-report.md: marked as example report
- docs/subagents/README.md: updated description and file structure

fix: sync `class_names` from dataset after training so `predict()` returns correct labels (#816)

* sync class_names from dataset after training so predict() returns correct labels (#509)

After trainer.fit(), RFDETR.train() now syncs RFDETRDataModule.class_names
back to self.model.class_names so that predict() returns dataset labels
instead of falling through to COCO defaults.

BestModelCallback._save_checkpoint() now enriches TrainConfig with dataset
class names (regular and EMA paths) before saving, so reloaded checkpoints
also carry the correct label mapping.

Five regression tests added covering both the in-memory sync and the
checkpoint serialisation paths.

- Updated `class_names` checks to use `is None` instead of truthiness in multiple paths (e.g., training sync, checkpoint saving) to avoid overwriting empty lists.
- Added regression tests validating that explicit `class_names=[]` are not replaced by dataset defaults (regular and EMA checkpoint paths).
- Use `getattr` for `config` and `datamodule` to avoid potential attribute errors during `class_names` sync.

commit.message:
Fix missing point clouds for G4TessellatedSolid fixture (#114)

* Initial plan

* Fix missing point clouds for G4TessellatedSolid fixture

The tessellated tetrahedron's bounding-box centre (10, 15, 20) lies
outside the solid (10/20 + 15/30 + 20/40 = 1.5 > 1). FixtureComparisonOrigin
was returning this external point, so TraceRay called DistanceToIn() from
an exterior origin, resulting in empty native_hits / imported_hits arrays
and therefore no visible point clouds in the viewer.

Extend the existing G4Tet branch to also handle G4TessellatedSolid:
use the vertex centroid from vertices_mm, which is guaranteed to lie
inside any convex tessellated solid, so DistanceToOut is used and every
ray direction produces a hit.

Also update the Doxygen comment in fixture_ray_compare.hh to document
the new G4TessellatedSolid origin rule.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add runtime Inside() check for G4TessellatedSolid centroid origin

Split the combined G4Tet / G4TessellatedSolid branch so that:
- G4Tet keeps the unconditional vertex-centroid (always inside a
  non-degenerate tetrahedron, no runtime check needed).
- G4TessellatedSolid computes the vertex centroid then validates it
  with solid.Inside(): if the centroid is kOutside (possible for
  concave or non-convex meshes) fall back to BoundingBoxCenter.

Update the Doxygen comment in fixture_ray_compare.hh to accurately
describe both branches and the runtime fallback behaviour.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix flaky rx-state multiInstance test by yielding on CONFLICT retry (#8116)

* Initial plan

* fix: yield to event loop on CONFLICT retry in RxState._triggerWrite()

In multiInstance mode, the CONFLICT retry loop in _triggerWrite() could
spin in a tight loop without yielding to the event loop, preventing
cross-instance change events from propagating and updating the
_lastIdQuery cache. This caused the 'write with two states at once'
test to intermittently time out.

Adding promiseWait(0) after catching CONFLICT gives the event loop a
chance to process incoming events before retrying.

calls could mutate global_env without invalidating the caller's cache.

generation against the current one and fall back to HashMap on mismatch.

across nested calls, setters, and callbacks.

* Add Vue framework support with analyzers, rules, and detection

- Updated the frameworks index to include new framework analyzers for Next.js, Pydantic, React, SQLAlchemy, and Vue.
- Added detailed documentation for each framework, outlining zen principles, rules, and detector catalogs.
- Revised links in the languages index to point to the new framework documentation.
- Ensured consistency in formatting and structure across all framework documentation.

- Updated `generate_language_docs.py` to generate documentation for both languages and frameworks.

- Updated Vue detector mappings to include specific detector classes and their configurations, replacing the previous rule IDs with explicit detector bindings.

- Added tests for frontend framework detectors to validate rule violations across various frameworks including React, Vue, Angular, and Next.js.
- Implemented tests for Python framework detectors covering Pydantic, FastAPI, Django, and SQLAlchemy to ensure adherence to updated coding standards.
- Enhanced universal dogma tests to verify that all supported language mappings define explicit universal dogmas.

* Refactor detector bindings to use RuleDetectorBinding and enhance dogma analysis

- Replaced DetectorBinding with RuleDetectorBinding in Terraform, TOML, TypeScript, XML, and YAML language mappings.
- Introduced a helper function `_dogmas` to streamline the assignment of universal dogma IDs.
- Updated the Violation model to include rule_id, detector_id, and universal_dogma_ids fields.
- Added DogmaFinding and DogmaAnalysis models to aggregate universal dogma findings.
- Enhanced report generation to include a section for universal dogmas.
- Implemented tests for dogma analysis and ensured proper integration with existing analysis results.
- Adjusted existing tests to accommodate changes in detector binding and dogma handling.

* Refactor rule binding structure in language mappings

- Updated SQL, SVG, Terraform, TOML, TypeScript, XML, and YAML language mappings to replace `rule_ids` and `universal_dogma_ids` with a new `rules` structure using `RuleBinding`.
- Enhanced clarity and consistency across language detector configurations by encapsulating rule and dogma associations within `RuleBinding` objects.

Add bundle-preserving testing and projection perspective support across registry, server, reporting, CLI, and authored language/framework mappings.

commit.message:
Add Settings section to README with auto-generated `flyline --help` output (#281)

Adds a `Settings` section to the README explaining how flyline is
configured, with the `flyline --help` output kept automatically in sync
via a new GitHub Action.

### README
- New `# Settings` section (between Installation and Integrations) with
the full `flyline --help` output in a fenced code block
- Notes that flyline auto-registers tab completion for its own flags on
load (via `setup_autocompletion()`)
- Marker comments bracket the help block for automated replacement:
  ```
  <!-- FLYLINE_HELP_START -->
  ```
  Usage: flyline [OPTIONS]
  ...
  ```
  <!-- FLYLINE_HELP_END -->
  ```

### `flyline_help.txt`
Plain-text copy of `flyline --help` output committed to the repo root;
kept in sync by the Action.

### `docker/flyline_help.Dockerfile` + `docker/docker-bake.hcl`
New `extract-help-text` bake target: runs `flyline --help` inside an
interactive bash session (`bash -i -c`) with `NO_COLOR=1`, strips
residual ANSI via `sed`, and exports `flyline_help.txt` via a `scratch`
final stage. Reuses the existing `built-artifact` context pattern.

### `.github/workflows/update-settings-docs.yml`
New workflow (triggers on push to `master`) that:
1. Runs `docker buildx bake extract-help-text` to produce
`flyline_help.txt`
2. Replaces the `FLYLINE_HELP_START/END` block in `README.md` via an
inline Python `re.sub`
3. Creates/updates an `update-settings-docs` PR if anything changed —
same pattern as `generate-gif.yml`

AI mode improvements: timer, error display, Shift+Enter, gutter char, dparser formatting (#279)

Several UX and correctness improvements to AI mode, covering visual
consistency, error surfacing, and input ergonomics.

## Changes

- **Gutter char on both lines**: `▐`/` ` indicator now shown at the
start of both the description and command lines in `AiOutputSelection`,
not just the description.

- **Elapsed timer**: `AiMode` stores a `start_time` and renders
`Running: <cmd> [Ns]` — updates every ~30ms poll cycle.

- **Error surfacing**: Added `ContentMode::AiError { message, raw_output
}`. Both command failures (non-zero exit, spawn error) and JSON parse
failures now display a red `AI failed: <message>` with up to 5 lines of
raw output, instead of silently returning to Normal. Dismissed with
Escape or Enter.

- **Shift+Enter triggers AI**: New key binding mirrors Ctrl+I —
activates when `--ai-command` is configured and no AI command is already
running.

- **dparser syntax highlighting**: Each suggestion's command line is now
rendered through `DParser` + `format_buffer` for token-based styling
(quoted strings, etc.) instead of unstyled raw text.

- **Quote words with spaces in display**: The `Running: ...` line wraps
space-containing args in single quotes, with embedded `'` escaped via
`'\''`.

- **Refactor**: Ctrl+I and Shift+Enter share a new `start_ai_mode()`
helper.

feat: custom prompt animations via `flyline create-anim` (#271)

Adds user-defined prompt animations: named frame sequences that replace
their name in `PS1`/`RPS1`/`PS1_FILL` with the current animation frame
on every render.

## Usage

```bash
flyline create-anim --name COOL_SPINNER --fps 10 '\e[33m|' '/' '-' '\\'
# Then in PS1:
export PS1='[COOL_SPINNER] \$ '
```

Frames support any bash prompt escapes (e.g. `\u`, `\w`) and ANSI colour
sequences (e.g. `\e[33m`). Multiple `create-anim` calls accumulate
animations in settings across the session.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`** — `PromptAnimation { name, fps, frames }` struct;
`custom_animations: Vec<PromptAnimation>` field on `Settings`

- **`lib.rs`** — `create-anim` clap subcommand; validates `fps > 0`;
stores frames raw (no pre-processing)

- **`prompt_manager.rs`**
- Animation names are left untouched in raw prompt strings (no
placeholder substitution)
- Added `ProcessedAnimation` struct — each frame is processed through
the same `expand_prompt_string` pipeline used for PS1/RPS1/PS1_FILL at
construction time (resolving bash prompt escapes like `\u`/`\w` and ANSI
colour codes into ratatui `Span`s)
- `PromptManager::new` accepts `animations: &[PromptAnimation]` and
builds `processed_animations: Vec<ProcessedAnimation>`
- `format_prompt_line` uses `flat_map` over `expand_span`, which finds
animation names by direct string search and splices in the pre-processed
frame spans
- `get_frame_spans`: selects the frame index from
`now.timestamp_millis()` — inherits the existing `disable_animations`
freeze behaviour since `get_ps1_lines` already passes
`construction_time` when animations are disabled

- **`app/mod.rs`** — passes `&settings.custom_animations` to
`PromptManager::new`

Auto-close: track auto-inserted closing tokens and delete paired closer on backspace (#277)

Auto-close lacked memory of which closing tokens it inserted, so
backspacing an opener left behind an orphaned closer. This adds
end-to-end tracking of auto-inserted closers using the dparser token
cache.

## Changes

### `TokenAnnotation::IsClosing` — new `is_auto_inserted` field
- Changed from tuple variant `IsClosing(usize)` to struct variant
`IsClosing { opening_idx: usize, is_auto_inserted: bool }`
- Dparser always sets `is_auto_inserted: false`; only the editor sets it
`true`
- Added `Copy` to the derive list (all variant fields are `Copy`)

### `DParser` — `into_tokens()`
- New method to take ownership of the token `Vec` without cloning

### `App` — dparser token cache
- Added `dparser_tokens_cache: Vec<AnnotatedToken>` field alongside the
existing `formatted_buffer_cache`
- `on_possible_buffer_change` now: builds a `HashSet` of auto-inserted
byte positions from the old cache, transfers flags to newly-parsed
tokens by byte-start position (best-effort), then stores the new tokens

### Auto-insert marking
`insert_closing_char` returns `Option<usize>` (byte position of the
inserted closer). After `on_possible_buffer_change` rebuilds the cache,
`mark_auto_inserted_closing(pos)` stamps `is_auto_inserted = true` on
that token.

### Paired-delete on backspace
New `delete_auto_inserted_closing_if_present` checks whether the token
immediately to the right of the cursor is the `IsClosing {
is_auto_inserted: true }` counterpart of the token about to be deleted.
If so, `delete_forwards()` is called first. This is gated on
`!disable_auto_closing_char` and only fires on plain `Backspace`.

```
echo [|]   →  Backspace  →  echo     ✓  (both [ and ] removed)
echo [a|]  →  Backspace  →  echo [|] ✓  (only 'a' removed; closer not auto-inserted after content exists)
```

Flag transfer is best-effort: inserting characters inside a pair shifts
the closer's byte position and the flag is not carried forward — this is
acceptable per the spec.

isautoinserted flag.

feat: granular safety xfail separating safety from ray/inside failures (#108)

* feat: granular safety xfail separating safety from ray/inside failures

Addresses #104: safety distances are allowed to be conservative in
Geant4 navigation (a larger-than-exact value is valid), so mismatches
between native and OCCT-imported DistanceToOut(p)/DistanceToIn(p) do
not necessarily indicate a correctness bug.  Previously xfail was
all-or-nothing per fixture, meaning ray and inside failures were hidden
alongside safety ones.

Changes:
- FixtureExpectedFailure gains safety_enabled/safety_reason fields
  alongside the existing enabled/reason pair.  Only the two scalar
  safety codes (fixture.safety_in_distance_mismatch and
  fixture.safety_out_distance_mismatch) are demoted when safety_enabled
  is true; ray, inside, and volume errors remain hard failures.
- ReclassifyExpectedFailures now accepts FixtureExpectedFailure instead
  of a bare reason string and applies each policy independently.  All
  call sites in test_geometry_validation.cc and bench_navigator.cc are
  updated; the if-guard is absorbed into the function (no-op when both
  flags are false).
- G4UnionSolid and G4SubtractionSolid are marked safety_enabled only:
  Geant4's G4UnionSolid::DistanceToOut(p) returns the distance to a
  component solid boundary (which may be an interior face), a
  conservative but permitted value per Geant4 navigation semantics.
  OCCT computes the geometrically exact distance to the actual boolean
  boundary.  Ray and inside failures for these classes will still cause
  test failures, ensuring STEP geometry translation errors are caught.

commit.message:
fix: include flags_snapshot in all history records via recordRound()

Previously flags_snapshot was only set in god.js's tick handler, so
scrubbing the timeline to non-god ticks showed no flags in the ISO view.

Adding flags_snapshot to recordRound() ensures all callers (tick.js,
scheduler.js, god.js) always capture the current flags state at the
moment of snapshotting, without each caller needing to remember to set it.
